title: Sour Silky Saudi Lamb Sandwich
description: A tangy, silky lamb sandwich with a yogurt‑tahini sauce, fresh vegetables, and a hint of sumac, perfect for meal‑prep.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder
ingredients.amount: 450 g (1 lb) thinly sliced lamb shoulder

ingredients.name: Salt and pepper
ingredients.notes: Use 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp pepper as guideline

instructions: Pat the lamb thin slices dry and season with salt, pepper, sumac, smoked paprika, and minced garlic. Toss in 2 tbsp of lemon juice and 1 tbsp olive oil and let marinate for 20 minutes.
While the lamb marinates, slice the English muffins in half and toast them lightly in a dry skillet or toaster until golden.
In a small bowl, combine Greek yogurt, the remaining 1 tbsp lemon juice, tahini, a pinch of salt, and a splash of water if needed to thin the sauce. Whisk until silky and set aside.

Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat. Add a drizzle of olive oil and sear the lamb slices for 1–2 minutes per side until browned and the internal temperature reaches about 140 °F (60 °C) for a tender, medium‑rare finish.
To assemble, spread a generous layer of the yogurt‑tahini sauce on each toasted muffin half. Layer the seared lamb, diced tomato, and sliced onion on top.
Close the sandwich, cut in half, and serve immediately. For meal‑prep, store the toppings and bread separately, and reheat the lamb lightly before assembling.
Keep portions refrigerated and consume within 2–3 days.
